54	# Blueprint Writer
55	
56	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
57

59

You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.

65

67	# Blueprint Writer Directive

71

73	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex

79	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
80	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
81	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
82	- ...
83	
84	## Out of scope
85	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
86	
87	## References
88	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
89	- ...
90	
91	## Expected outcome
92	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
93	```
94	
95	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.

98	
99	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
100	
101	```latex
102	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
103	  \label{thm:some_label}
104	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
105	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
106	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174  (read from references/hartshorne-III-5.md)
107	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A morphism $f: X \to Y$ of schemes locally of finite
108	  % type is said to be smooth at $x \in X$ if there exist an open affine
109	  % neighborhood $V = \Spec B$ of $f(x)$ and an open affine neighborhood
110	  % $U = \Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \subset V$ such that ..."
111	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, III.5.1.}
112	  Informal statement of the theorem, in the project's notation.
113	\end{theorem}
114	
115	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Proof. We may assume $Y = \Spec B$ and
116	% $X = \Spec A$ are affine. Then $f$ corresponds to a ring homomorphism
117	% $\varphi: B \to A$, and $f$ is smooth at $x$ if and only if ..."
118	\begin{proof}
119	  \uses{thm:another_result}
120	  Step-by-step informal proof, in the project's notation. Reference blueprint labels
121	  with \uses{...} so the dependency graph stays accurate.
122	\end{proof}
123	```
124	
125	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
126	
127	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
128	
129	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
130	
131	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
132	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
133	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.
134	   - **Original notation preserved.** If the source writes $\mathcal{O}_X^*$ and the project uses $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$, the verbatim quote keeps $\mathcal{O}_X^*$. The project-notation restatement happens in the prose body that follows, NOT in the quote.
135	   - **Every word, every symbol.** No paraphrase, no "for brevity" abbreviation. The quote is the anti-hallucination signal — a writer who actually opened the source can paste; a writer reconstructing from memory cannot reproduce the source's exact words.
136	3. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` line** — first line of the prose body. Renders in the PDF so a mathematician reading the typeset blueprint sees the citation at a glance.
137	
138	**For proof blocks**: include a `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` LaTeX comment **immediately before** the `\begin{proof}` environment (NOT inside it). Same verbatim rules — original language, original notation, every word. The informal proof body inside `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` is the project's restated version in project notation, what the prover formalizes.

142	**For Archon-original / project-bespoke results** (the directive does not name an external source for this block): the source lines are omitted — the block stands on the proof sketch alone.

144	**The hard rule, explicit:**

146	> **You may NEVER write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from training memory.** Every such line must be backed by a local file under `references/` that you have **opened and read in this session**, and the verbatim quote must be a character-by-character copy of text from that file.

43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88

344	\section{Milne Theorem 3.1: codimension-\(\geq 2\) extension}
345	\label{sec:milne_thm31}
346	
347	The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
348	variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
349	point.
350	
351	\begin{theorem}

363	  % \(\geq 2\).}"

366	  \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. Then the
367	  indeterminacy locus \(Z(f)\) (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension

406	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-\(1\) components.} Suppose for contradiction that \(Z(f)\)
407	  contains a prime divisor \(W \subseteq X\) (i.e.\ a codim-\(1\) irreducible component).
408	  Let \(\eta \in W\) be its generic point, so \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). By
409	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
410	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\). Any representative
411	  \((U, \varphi_U) \in f\) has \(U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset\) and so determines
412	  a \(\bar k\)-algebra map from the function field \(K(Y)\) of \(Y\) to \(K(X)\): explicitly,
413	  \(\varphi_U\) defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y\) (since
414	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))\) is the generic point of \(X\)). By the valuative criterion
415	  of properness applied to the complete variety \(Y\)
416	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
417	  extends to a morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y\). Standard
418	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood \(V\) of \(\eta\) in
419	  \(X\) on which \(f\) has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
420	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)\(, contradicting \)\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)\(. Hence no codim-\)1$
421	  component of \(X\) lies in \(Z(f)\), i.e.\ \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\).
422

424	  \(\Rightarrow\) extension" half).} Suppose now that \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)
425	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) under the additional input
426	  that ``\(f\) is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
427	  point \(x \in Z(f)\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2\). By
428	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
429	  \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\), we have
430	  \(\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2\). The sheaf
431	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on the punctured neighbourhood \(V \smallsetminus \{x\}\)
432	  (any small affine neighbourhood \(V \ni x\)) has \(H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\)
433	  and \(H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\) (the depth-\(\geq 2\) characterisation of
434	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for

437	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on \(V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) extend uniquely to sections on \(V\),
438	  yielding a regular representative of \(f\) on \(V\). Hence \(x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\),
439	  contradicting \(x \in Z(f)\). Therefore \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) and \(f\) extends to a
440	  regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X \to Y\).

442	  Uniqueness of \(\widetilde f\) is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
443	  principle: two morphisms \(X \to Y\) that agree on the dense open \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\)

450	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
451	  Step~2 uses the depth-\(\geq 2\) extension property at a codim-\(\geq 2\) regular
452	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
453	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension \(d\)
454	  \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay of depth \(d\)). For our application the source \(X\) is a
455	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
456	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.

459	  \(C \times C\) for a smooth projective curve \(C\)), the codim-\(\geq 2\) closed points of
460	  \(X\) are exactly the closed points of \(X\) (because \(\dim X = 2\)), and at each such
461	  point \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular of dimension \(2\), hence Cohen--Macaulay of

473	this forces the locus to be empty.

609	  divisor of \(X \times X\) either contains \(\Delta_X\) or meets it in pure codimension

611	  applied to the local equation of \(\Delta_X\) inside \(X \times X\)).

633	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
634	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on \(X \times X\). None of these has a characteristic
635	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
636	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the

640	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
641	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
642	
643	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
644	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
645	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
646	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
647	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
648	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
649	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
650	
651	\begin{theorem}
652	
653	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
654	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
655	  
656	  % NOTE (iter-179 review, lvb-checker `codimone-iter179` corrective):
657	  % `\lean{...}` pin DETACHED (Path D2 option (b) per the lvb-checker). The
658	  % substantive Hartshorne-II.6 valuative reformulation (the iff with
659	  % ord_W >= 0) below is the *intended* statement; the iter-179 Lane D
660	  % `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` decl is only a 2-LOC
661	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` reshuffle that does NOT prove the
662	  % ord_W content, so re-pinning `\lean{...}` to it would falsely claim
663	  % kernel-clean formalization of the substantive Hartshorne iff. The
664	  % weaker reshuffle proven this iter will be pinned to its own light
665	  % `\begin{lemma}` block (working title `lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle`)
666	  % by an iter-180+ blueprint-writer dispatch; the substantive Hartshorne
667	  % version stays pinned here but unlinked until a future
668	  % `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg` lemma carries
669	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.order` machinery (pullback `RationalMap →
670	  % function-field ring map`, not yet in Mathlib at b80f227). See
671	  % task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-codimone-iter179.md +
672	  % proof-journal/sessions/session_179/recommendations.md REC-14.
673	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation \(v_Y\) associated to a
674	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}\) as the
675	  % subring of \(K(X)\) on which \(v_Y \geq 0\)) (read from
676	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
677	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
678	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
679	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
680	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
681	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).
682	  % Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has a \emph{zero} along
683	  % \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\) has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of
684	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
685	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
686	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
687	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
688	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
689	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
690	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
691	  \begin{enumerate}
692	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
693	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
694	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
695	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
696	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
697	      satisfying
698	      \[
699	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
700	      \]
701	  \end{enumerate}
702	
703	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
704	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
705	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
706	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
707	\end{theorem}
708	
709	\begin{proof}
710	  
711	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
712	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
713	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
714	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
715	  \[
716	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
717	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
718	  \]
719	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
720	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
721	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
722	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
723	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
724	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
725	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
726	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
727	
728	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
729	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
730	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
731	\end{proof}
732

737	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in

747	\begin{lemma}
748	
749	\leanok
750	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
751	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
752	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
753	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
754	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
755	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
756	  following are equivalent:
757	  \begin{enumerate}
758	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
759	      \(W\)).
760	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
761	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
762	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
763	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
764	  \end{enumerate}
765	\end{lemma}
766	
767	\begin{proof}
768	
769	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
770	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
771	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
772	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
773	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
774	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
775	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
776	  the unfolded definition.
777	\end{proof}

29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma

69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap

185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/

637	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
638	
639	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
640	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
641	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
642	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
643	
644	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
645	
646	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
647	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
648	
649	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
650	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
651	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
652	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
653	
654	The proof combines two steps:
655	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
656	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
657	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
658	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
659	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
660	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
661	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
662	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
663	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
664	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
665	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
666	
667	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
668	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
669	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
670	
671	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
672	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
673	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
674	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
675	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
676	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
677	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
678	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
679	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
680	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
681	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
682	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
683	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
684	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
685	  sorry

687	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
688	
689	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
690	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
691	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
692	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
693	
694	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
695	
696	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
697	codim 1.**
698	
699	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
700	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
701	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
702	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
703	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
704	
705	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
706	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
707	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
708	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
709	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
710	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
711	
712	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
713	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/

716	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
717	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
718	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
719	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
720	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
721	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
722	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]

\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}

The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a

locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of

axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open

\begin{lemma}
  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
  \(S_M\)-module.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  The canonical comparison map
  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
  the conclusion.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
  the relative dimension]
  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  Mathlib's
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
  ranks equal \(n\), so
  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
\end{proof}
